hero.headline: How does Kentucky classify workers as employees or contractors?
hero.subhead: Two tests, one state: KRS 341 ABC for UI, KRS 342 common-law for workers' comp.

classification_framework.ui_test: ABC test under KRS 341.050 — all three prongs required
classification_framework.workers_comp_test: Common-law control test under KRS Chapter 342
classification_framework.irs_alignment: Common-law test tracks IRS Publication 15-A; ABC test does not (Prong B has no IRS equivalent)
classification_framework.kedc_enforcement: Kentucky Education and Development Cabinet — can issue retroactive employee determinations

misclassification_exposure.local_occ_tax_counties: 87 of 120 counties plus hundreds of cities

abc_test_prongs.requirement: Free from direction and control in performance of services, under contract and in fact
Services performed outside usual course of business, OR outside all places of business
Customarily engaged in independently established trade, occupation, profession, or business

pricing.model: Single fixed rate, Zero FX mark-up

faq_schema.q: How does Kentucky classify workers for unemployment insurance?
faq_schema.a: Kentucky uses the ABC test under KRS 341.050. All three prongs must be satisfied: (A) free from direction and control, (B) services outside usual course of business or outside the company's premises, and (C) individually engaged in an independently established trade or business.

faq_schema.q: What is the penalty for misclassifying a worker in Kentucky?
faq_schema.a: Back UI contributions on the $12,000 2026 wage base, a 25% penalty under KRS 341.330, interest at 1.5% per month, back state income tax at 3.5%, and occupational licence fees across applicable counties.

faq_schema.q: Is a Kentucky contractor who passes the IRS test also safe for UI purposes?
faq_schema.a: Not automatically. KRS 341's Prong B (outside usual course of business) has no IRS equivalent. An IRS-defensible relationship can still fail Prong B if the contractor's work falls within the company's core business function.

faq_schema.q: Does Kentucky use a different test for workers' comp vs UI?
faq_schema.a: Yes. Kentucky is a dual-test state. KRS 341.050 (ABC test) governs UI; KRS Chapter 342 (common-law control) governs workers' compensation. Satisfying one does not satisfy the other.

meta.title: Kentucky Worker Classification State Test | KRS 341 ABC Test 2026
meta.description: Kentucky uses a dual test: ABC test under KRS 341.050 for UI, common-law control under KRS 342 for workers' comp. Misclassification triggers 25% penalty + 1.5%/month.
